titleStmt.title: Letter from Captain G. Stratford-Burton to the Under-Secretary for Ireland, 12 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: G. Stratford-Burton

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was sent by Captain G. Stratford-Burton, the garrison adjutant for Ship St. Barracks and Dublin District. The letter was addressed to the under-secretary for Ireland. Up until May this position was occupied by Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). However, in May Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising. He was soon replaced by Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). In this letter Stratford-Burton informs under-secretary that he is forwarding him the twelfth list of casualties incurred in connection with the Sinn Féin insurrection.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/8285/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Subject:- Sinn Fein insurrection. DG. CR. No. 45/16 The, Under Secretary for Ireland, Upper Castle yard. DUBLIN. Sir, I have the honor to forward herewith the 12th
List of Causalities incurred in connection with the Sinn
Fein Insurrection. I have the honor to be,
Your obedient servant,
G. Stratford-Burton
Capt. G.A. The Colonel.
Commanding Dublin Garrison
Lr. Castle Yard. DUBLIN. 12th May, 1916.
Dublin District. The following is a supplementary list of Casualties:
KING GEORGE VTH HOSPITAL
1056. Gn. Cartlidge. 1st North Midland R.F.A. G.S W. Hand & Thigh.
3629. Pte. Roberts G, 2/6 South Staffs. ' Both Thighs
4877. ' Shaw F 2/8 Sherwood Foresters. ' Hand Right.
3022. 'Greaves M 2/8 ' ' ' Thigh Right.
ROYAL CITY OF DUBLIN HOSPITAL. BAGGOT STREET.
5617. Pte Bradford J. H. 2/7 Sherwood Foresters. DEAD
5510. ' Ellis F. 2/7 ' ' G.S W Arm left.
3111. ' Goddard 2/7 ' ' ' Wrist Left.
5664 ' Miller T.H. 2/7 ' ' ' Thorax.
5445 ' Olley B 2/7 ' ' ' Ankle left.
5697 ' Wilkins WC. 2/7 ' ' Collapse
2896. ' Scotney 2/5 Leicester Regiment G.S W. Thorax & Arm Right
Sgt Foster C.R. ' Temple.
DOCTOR STEVENS' HOSPITAL X Captain Charlton R. 2/7 Sherwood Foresters. G. S.W. Leg Left
5698 Sgt Joliffe R. 10th Res. Regt Calvalry ' Thigh Left.
1223 Supper Holley F. Royal Engineers ' Scalp
TEMPLE HILL AUX> HOSPITAL. 3880. Pte Chappelle. West Riding Regt. G.S.W Finger
2603. Sapper noore L. R.E. Att. 69th Mid. Div. ' Hand
DUBLIN CASTLE RED CROSS HOSPITAL.
5141. Pte Jones T. 3rd Pembroke Yeo. Splinter Eye Right
MILITARY HOSPITAL PORTOBELLO. 1005. Pte hutchinson 2nd Gr Bn R.I. Fus. Bayonet Wound Hand
MEATH HOSPITAL. 9594. Sgt. Harris J. 2/5 South Staffs. G.S.W Thigh Right The undernoted were casualties dates unknown and were buried in
the grounds of the Parochial Hall, Northumberland Road, Ballsbridge:
Sgt Fletcher 2/6 South Staffs.
Pte. barrett 2/6 ' '
See list 13
questionable reading Captain R.A.N.C for Lieut- Colonel R.A.M.C.
i/e King George V Hospital.
Dublin. 12th may, 1916.
